title: Introduction to Node.js and the Express Framework

questions.sentence: Nodejs is a runtime environment that allows you to run JavaScript on the server outside of a web browser
This enables front end developers to use their existing JavaScript skills to build fullstack applications
To make serverside development even simpler many developers use Expressjs a minimalist web framework for Nodejs
Expres provides a set of features to handle common back end tasks like routing which determines how an application responds to a specific URL request
For example using Expres you can easily create a route that sends back product information when a user visits apiproducts
Understanding Nodejs and Expres is a significant milestone for any front end developer looking to understand the complete application lifecycle
